title: Hidden away in an Australian Discord, a

messages.author: James Cridland
messages.content_html: Agree, that seems sensible.
messages.reply_html:
Christopher A. Wichura
I would not remove the setting. There will always be people outside the standard use case. But in principal, I agree with your approach to CR. It's something we are doing here manually in a still fledgling MC region. I think best would be to add an "auto" option to the CR setting. Let that be the default, and follow your strategy. But if the user needs to override it, they can.

messages.author: Christopher A. Wichura
messages.text: Yes. The LoRa frame header is always sent CR8 and includes the CR for the rest of the frame. So senders can set independent CR, even on a per-packet basis. (E.g., if you knew you can use CR5 for a "close by" node and then needed CR8 for a "far away" node, that's entirely possible -- but not something MeshCore currently supports). (edited)
messages.content_html: Yes. The LoRa frame header is always sent CR8 and includes the CR for the rest of the frame. So senders can set independent CR, even on a per-packet basis. (E.g., if you knew you can use CR5 for a "close by" node and then needed CR8 for a "far away" node, that's entirely possible -- but not something MeshCore currently supports). (edited)

messages.author: Flesh Kimmeh
messages.text: Thanks for explaining. I'm working on a little personal project for funsies, and I can definitely make use of this. I suggested above that if meshcore wanted to adopt this, then one strategy could be to increase the CR each time it has to resend a message, and it could try decreasing it over time, and thereby find a good balance fuzzily. I will try this in my project and report back if I learn anything interesting
messages.content_html: Thanks for explaining. I'm working on a little personal project for funsies, and I can definitely make use of this. I suggested above that if meshcore wanted to adopt this, then one strategy could be to increase the CR each time it has to resend a message, and it could try decreasing it over time, and thereby find a good balance fuzzily. I will try this in my project and report back if I learn anything interesting

messages.author: James Cridland
messages.text: Yes. You can change the CR to whatever you like - CR 8 takes 40% longer to broadcast than CR 5 from what I understand. Hence why you should set it to “as low as you can get away with”.
messages.content_html: Yes. You can change the CR to whatever you like - CR 8 takes 40% longer to broadcast than CR 5 from what I understand. Hence why you should set it to “as low as you can get away with”.
